title: Real-Robot Demos
description: 真机 demo queue for Wuji / Franka / dexterous-hand experiments
summary: This Engineering card owns the physical real-robot demo list. DexGello remains the data/control dependency layer for q_arm, T_hand, q_finger, MANUS/VIVE logging, and cuRobo where needed; demo scenarios, consumables, safety gates, owners, and acceptance evidence live here.

rows.owner: Yuhang & Jingxiang / Boris / DexGello dependency support
rows.hardware: Franka Research 3 + Wuji Hand + toy knife; DexGello logger and cuRobo are dependencies
rows.object_setup: Slime clay as simulated meat; blunt-edge toy knife; cutting board; washable desktop protection mat; fixture or tray; washable work area
rows.todo: Procure or confirm toy knife, slime clay, cutting board, and washable desktop mat; then run low-speed Stage 0/1 with E-stop watcher, workspace limits, video, q_arm / T_hand / q_finger / Wuji state logging.
rows.notes: No real knife, real meat, or high-speed cutting without separate approval. The demo row lives here; DexGello supplies the data/control stack.

rows.owner: Master student TBD / Zheng Yuhang for Daimon gripper timing
rows.hardware: Robot arm + printable or custom block-extraction gripper + Daimon tactile sensors
rows.object_setup: Jenga tower or custom block stack; target-block stickers / colored tape; non-slip base or anti-slip mat; tactile/VTA/VLA failure labels
rows.todo: Procure or confirm Jenga/custom blocks, target markers, and non-slip base; assign owner and confirm whether the Daimon-side gripper package is ready and when it can reach Singapore.
rows.notes: Track slip, contact stability, lateral force, tower wobble, collapse risk, and recovery; keep as a compact true-robot benchmark.

rows.demo: Wuji hand + Aloha-style 3M anti-slip tape rubbing plastic bag
rows.hardware: Wuji dexterous hand with Aloha-style 3M anti-slip tape on contact surfaces
rows.object_setup: Plastic bag rubbing task; 3M anti-slip tape if not already available; high-contrast tabletop/background mat; camera view should show contact patch and bag deformation
rows.todo: Confirm tape spec and placement, procure high-contrast mat, protect fingertips/cables, then run low-speed rubbing demo with video and state logs.
rows.notes: Reference Xiaohongshu plastic-bag demo link is recorded in project references; goal is the cross-embodiment brain story from gripper to dexterous hand.

rows.hardware: Wuji or dexterous hand + Lego brick separator + safe plastic small tools + Lego/MOC pieces
rows.object_setup: Assemble / disassemble first-round large-particle Lego/MOC structures using tool manipulation; later move to small pieces if stable
rows.todo: Procure or confirm Lego brick separator, safe plastic hammer/plier-style tools, and large-particle first-round Lego/MOC parts; compare Singapore shipping and wait for purchase confirmation.
rows.notes: Track both procurement and the first pick-use-tool-assemble/disassemble video/log evidence; do not mark done at link collection only.

rows.demo: Long-horizon failure-data demo: sweep object into dustpan
rows.hardware: Franka + Wuji or simpler dexterous-hand/gripper tool-holding setup; existing tabletop broom/dustpan toy from Boris' COM2 seat
rows.object_setup: Light tabletop object or debris proxy; existing small broom/dustpan toy to transfer from COM2 seat to ME; camera view of object progress and tool contact
rows.todo: Have a human transfer the existing small broom/dustpan toy from Boris' COM2 seat to ME; design safe setup, then run dry-run and low-speed robot trial with phase labels: grasp broom, align object, sweep, enter dustpan, settle.
rows.notes: Primary value is long-horizon stage importance for failure-data learning: failed sweeps still contain learnable approach/grasp/alignment phases, while bad contact or missed dustpan stages should be low-weight failure evidence.

rows.demo: Long-horizon failure-data demo: two 90-degree cube turns
rows.hardware: Wuji or dexterous hand; existing cube from Boris' COM2 seat if suitable, otherwise low-friction marked cube or safe Rubik-style cube
rows.object_setup: Existing cube to transfer from COM2 seat to ME and inspect for torque/visibility; stable table/fixture; camera view of finger contact and face orientation
rows.todo: Have a human transfer the existing cube from Boris' COM2 seat to ME; inspect friction/markers, then define two-step 90-degree turn task, safe force limits, orientation labels, and low-speed trial that records success/failure after each quarter-turn.
rows.notes: Use this as a compact long-horizon manipulation sequence: pregrasp, first 90-degree turn, regrasp/stabilize, second 90-degree turn. Good for testing whether the metric separates useful early phases from failed final rotation.

details: Scope rule: this card owns demo scenarios, physical objects, consumables, safety gates, and acceptance evidence. DexGello owns reusable data/control dependencies such as GELLO arm state, MANUS/Wuji finger state, VIVE hand pose, cuRobo planning, and episode logging.

Procurement rule: Taobao/Lego/demo-kit candidate links are TODO evidence only. Do not mark procurement done until purchase route, delivery destination, receipt/procurement record, and arrival/fit check are recorded.

Safety rule: every demo starts with read-only or simulation/dry-run, then low-speed robot motion with workspace limits, cable checks, and a dedicated E-stop watcher before collecting publishable evidence.

Failure-data link 2026-06-13: broom-to-dustpan and two 90-degree cube-turn demos are added specifically as longer-horizon data sources for DexGello learn-from-failure metrics. They should log phase boundaries, intervention/takeover, object/contact progress, and which failed stages should not be imitated.

Procurement update 2026-06-16: v0 demo shopping list now covers toy-knife slime, Jenga target marking / anti-slip base, 3M plastic-bag visibility mat, Lego large-particle tool operation, and two 1-to-3 USB-C-output data/charging cables for VIVE Tracker / MANUS. Do not re-buy the existing COM2-seat props unless fit check fails.

Existing-prop transfer note 2026-06-16: Boris has data-collection props at his COM2 seat, including a small broom/dustpan toy, cube, ping-pong paddle, and related toys. These need human transfer to ME; they are not procurement items.

references.title: Wuji plastic-bag rubbing reference from Xiaohongshu
references.notes: User-provided reference for Wuji dexterous-hand plastic-bag rubbing demo with Aloha-style 3M anti-slip tape.

references.title: Taobao Lego disassembly tools candidate
references.notes: 拆装标准乐板高小颗粒积木拆卸辅助工具拆件器锤子钳子起件器零件; compare Singapore shipping and total price before ordering.

references.title: Taobao bulk Lego / MOC pieces candidate
references.notes: 正品乐高 lego 小颗粒散件 / MOC 二手乐高积木论斤卖随机称散装积木; compare Singapore shipping and total price before ordering.

risks_decisions: Do not let demo ownership drift back into DexGello. DexGello can provide dependencies and logs, but this Engineering card is the source of truth for scenario rows and open demo TODOs.
Plastic-bag rubbing depends on choosing a safe 3M anti-slip tape placement that improves grip without damaging Wuji fingertips, blocking sensors, or snagging cables.

Lego / tool-manipulation procurement needs human purchase confirmation. First-round parts should bias toward large-particle bricks and safe plastic tools before small-particle challenges. Link discovery and shipping comparison are progress, not completion.
Long-horizon failure-data demos should not be judged only by final success. They need per-stage labels so DexGello can learn useful pre-failure segments without imitating the actual failed action.
All demos need explicit low-speed safety gates, workspace limits, cable routing, object fixture cleanup, and logged pass/fail evidence before being treated as done.

timeline.target_note: Keep each demo row independently trackable; avoid hiding physical scenario work inside DexGello control-stack tasks.
